title: Helix, turn, sheet — all in one strand
author: OpenDiscover
blurb: Long before AlphaFold, biochemists could already guess a protein's shape from its letters. Chou and Fasman noticed that each amino acid has a personality: alanine loves to coil into an α-helix, valine prefers to lie flat in a β-sheet, and proline with glycine tends to snap the chain into a turn. This little designer peptide puts all three on display — nine alanines that spiral up, an N-P-G-K hinge that turns the corner, and nine valines that flatten into a strand. Slide any sequence in and watch the propensities vote, residue by residue, on whether the backbone helixes, sheets, or bends. Deterministic: the same sequence always folds to the same call.
